timedatamodel
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

DataPoint

class timedatamodel.DataPoint(timestamp, value)[source]
Parameters:

  • timestamp (datetime)

  • value (float | None)

timestamp: datetime

Alias for field number 0

value: float | None

Alias for field number 1

TimeSeries

class timedatamodel.TimeSeries(frequency, *, timezone='UTC', timestamps=None, values=None, data=None, name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, location=None, timeseries_type=TimeSeriesType.FLAT, attributes=None, index_names=None)[source]

Bases: _TimeSeriesBase

Parameters:

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • timezone (str)

  • timestamps (list[datetime] | list[tuple[datetime, ...]] | None)

  • values (list[float | None] | None)

  • data (list[DataPoint] | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • location (GeoLocation | GeoArea | None)

  • timeseries_type (TimeSeriesType)

  • attributes (dict[str, str])

  • index_names (list[str] | None)

frequency: Frequency
timezone: str
name: str | None
unit: str | None
description: str | None
data_type: DataType | None
location: GeoLocation | GeoArea | None
timeseries_type: TimeSeriesType
attributes: dict[str, str]
property values: list[float | None]
property column_names: tuple[str, ...]
property has_missing: bool

True if any value is None.

coverage_bar()[source]

Return a displayable coverage bar.

Return type:

CoverageBar

property pint_unit

Resolve the unit string to a pint.Unit object.

convert_unit(target_unit)[source]

Return a new TimeSeries with values converted to target_unit.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

target_unit (str)

equals(other)[source]

Full structural equality (all metadata + NaN-aware values).

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

other (object)

head(n=5)[source]

Return a new TimeSeries with the first n points.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

n (int)

tail(n=5)[source]

Return a new TimeSeries with the last n points.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

n (int)

copy()[source]

Return a shallow copy (timestamps and values lists are new).

Return type:

TimeSeries

property arr: ndarray

Shorthand for to_numpy().

property df: pd.DataFrame

Shorthand for to_pandas_dataframe().

to_numpy()[source]

Return values as a 1D numpy float64 array (None -> nan).

Return type:

ndarray

to_pandas_dataframe()[source]

Return a pandas DataFrame with DatetimeIndex or MultiIndex.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

to_pd_df()[source]

Alias for to_pandas_dataframe().

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

to_pl_df()[source]

Alias for to_polars_dataframe().

to_polars_dataframe()[source]

Return a polars DataFrame with timestamp and value columns.

classmethod from_pandas(df, frequency=None, value_column=None, *, timezone='UTC', name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, location=None, timeseries_type=TimeSeriesType.FLAT, attributes=None)[source]

Create a TimeSeries from a pandas DataFrame.

Supports DatetimeIndex (single-index) and MultiIndex (multi-index). If frequency is None, frequency and timezone are auto-inferred.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame)

  • frequency (Frequency | None)

  • value_column (str | None)

  • timezone (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • location (Location | None)

  • timeseries_type (TimeSeriesType)

  • attributes (dict[str, str] | None)

classmethod from_polars(df, frequency, timestamp_column='timestamp', value_column=None, *, timezone='UTC', name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, location=None, timeseries_type=TimeSeriesType.FLAT, attributes=None)[source]

Create a TimeSeries from a polars DataFrame.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • timestamp_column (str)

  • value_column (str | None)

  • timezone (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • location (GeoLocation | GeoArea | None)

  • timeseries_type (TimeSeriesType)

  • attributes (dict[str, str] | None)

update_from_pandas(df, value_column=None, inplace=False)[source]

Update a TimeSeries from a pandas DataFrame.

By default returns a new TimeSeries. With inplace=True the current instance is mutated and None is returned.

Return type:

TimeSeries | None

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame)

  • value_column (str | None)

  • inplace (bool)

update_df(df, value_column=None)[source]

Shorthand for update_from_pandas(df) — always returns a new TimeSeries.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame)

  • value_column (str | None)

update_arr(arr)[source]

Create a new TimeSeries with arr as values, keeping timestamps and metadata.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

arr (ndarray)

to_json()[source]

Serialize to a JSON string (timestamps as ISO-8601 strings).

Return type:

str

classmethod from_json(s, frequency=None, *, timezone=None, name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, location=None, timeseries_type=None, attributes=None)[source]

Reconstruct a TimeSeries from a JSON string produced by to_json().

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • s (str)

  • frequency (Frequency | None)

  • timezone (str | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • location (GeoLocation | GeoArea | None)

  • timeseries_type (TimeSeriesType | None)

  • attributes (dict[str, str] | None)

to_csv(path)[source]

Write timestamps and values to a CSV file.

Return type:

None

Parameters:

path (str | Path)

classmethod from_csv(path, frequency, *, timezone='UTC', name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, location=None, timeseries_type=TimeSeriesType.FLAT, attributes=None)[source]

Read a TimeSeries from a CSV file produced by to_csv().

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • path (str | Path)

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • timezone (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • location (GeoLocation | GeoArea | None)

  • timeseries_type (TimeSeriesType)

  • attributes (dict[str, str] | None)

validate()[source]

Return a list of validation warnings.

Return type:

list[str]

apply_pandas(func)[source]

Apply a pandas transformation, preserving metadata and auto-detecting frequency.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

func (Callable[['pd.DataFrame'], 'pd.DataFrame'])

apply_numpy(func)[source]

Apply a numpy transformation to values, keeping timestamps and frequency unchanged.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

func (Callable[[ndarray], ndarray])

static merge(series)[source]

Combine multiple univariate TimeSeries into a TimeSeriesTable.

All series must share the same timestamps (same length and values).

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

series (list[TimeSeries])

TimeSeriesTable

class timedatamodel.TimeSeriesTable(frequency, *, timezone='UTC', timestamps=None, values, names=None, units=None, descriptions=None, data_types=None, locations=None, timeseries_types=None, attributes=None, index_names=None)[source]

Bases: _TimeSeriesBase

Parameters:

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • timezone (str)

  • timestamps (list[datetime] | list[tuple[datetime, ...]] | None)

  • values (np.ndarray | list)

  • names (list[str | None])

  • units (list[str | None])

  • descriptions (list[str | None])

  • data_types (list[DataType | None])

  • locations (list[GeoLocation | GeoArea | None])

  • timeseries_types (list[TimeSeriesType])

  • attributes (list[dict[str, str]])

  • index_names (list[str] | None)

frequency: Frequency
timezone: str
names: list[str | None]
units: list[str | None]
descriptions: list[str | None]
data_types: list[DataType | None]
locations: list[GeoLocation | GeoArea | None]
timeseries_types: list[TimeSeriesType]
attributes: list[dict[str, str]]
property values: ndarray
property n_columns: int
property column_names: tuple[str, ...]
property has_missing: bool

True if any value is NaN.

coverage_bar()[source]

Return a displayable coverage bar.

Return type:

CoverageBar

convert_unit(target_unit, column=None)[source]

Return a new table with values converted to target_unit.

Parameters:

  • target_unit (str) – The unit to convert to.

  • column (int, str, or None) – If None, convert all columns. Otherwise convert only the specified column (by index or name).

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

filter_columns_by_location(center, radius_km)[source]

Keep only columns within radius_km of center.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

  • center (GeoLocation)

  • radius_km (float)

filter_columns_by_area(area)[source]

Keep only columns inside area.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

area (GeoArea)

nearest_columns(target, n=1)[source]

Keep the n nearest columns to target.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

  • target (GeoLocation)

  • n (int)

equals(other)[source]

Full structural equality (all metadata + NaN-aware values).

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

other (object)

select_column(col)[source]

Extract a single column as a univariate TimeSeries.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

col (int | str)

to_univariate_list()[source]

Convert to a list of univariate TimeSeries, one per column.

Return type:

list[‘TimeSeries’]

head(n=5)[source]

Return a new TimeSeriesTable with the first n points.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

n (int)

tail(n=5)[source]

Return a new TimeSeriesTable with the last n points.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

n (int)

copy()[source]

Return a shallow copy (timestamps list and values array are new).

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

property arr: ndarray

Shorthand for to_numpy().

property df: pd.DataFrame

Shorthand for to_pandas_dataframe().

to_numpy()[source]

Return values as a 2D numpy float64 array.

Return type:

ndarray

to_pandas_dataframe()[source]

Return a pandas DataFrame with multiple value columns.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

to_pd_df()[source]

Alias for to_pandas_dataframe().

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

to_pl_df()[source]

Alias for to_polars_dataframe().

to_polars_dataframe()[source]

Return a polars DataFrame with timestamp and value columns.

classmethod from_pandas(df, frequency=None, *, timezone='UTC', names=None, units=None, descriptions=None, data_types=None, locations=None, timeseries_types=None, attributes=None)[source]

Create a TimeSeriesTable from a pandas DataFrame.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame)

  • frequency (Frequency | None)

  • timezone (str)

  • names (list[str | None] | None)

  • units (list[str | None] | None)

  • descriptions (list[str | None] | None)

  • data_types (list[DataType | None] | None)

  • locations (list[Location | None] | None)

  • timeseries_types (list[TimeSeriesType] | None)

  • attributes (list[dict[str, str]] | None)

update_df(df)[source]

Create a new TimeSeriesTable from a DataFrame, preserving metadata.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

df (pd.DataFrame)

update_arr(arr)[source]

Create a new TimeSeriesTable with arr as values, keeping timestamps and metadata.

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

arr (ndarray)

Serialize to a JSON string (timestamps as ISO-8601 strings).

Return type:

str

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

None

path (str | Path)

classmethod from_csv(path, frequency, *, timezone='UTC', names=None, units=None, descriptions=None, data_types=None, locations=None, timeseries_types=None, attributes=None)[source]

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

TimeSeriesCube

class timedatamodel.TimeSeriesCube(frequency, *, timezone='UTC', name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, attributes=None, dimensions=None, values)[source]

Bases: object

Parameters:

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • timezone (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • attributes (dict[str, str])

  • dimensions (list[Dimension])

  • values (np.ndarray | np.ma.MaskedArray)

frequency: Frequency
timezone: str
name: str | None
unit: str | None
description: str | None
data_type: DataType | None
attributes: dict[str, str]
dimensions: list[Dimension]
property shape: tuple[int, ...]
property ndim: int
property dim_names: tuple[str, ...]
property coords: dict[str, list]
property primary_time_dim: Dimension
property begin: datetime | float | str | None
property end: datetime | float | str | None
property has_missing: bool

Return type:

TimeSeriesCube | ‘TimeSeriesTable’ | ‘TimeSeries

Return type:

TimeSeriesCube | ‘TimeSeriesTable’ | ‘TimeSeries

Return type:

TimeSeries

TimeSeriesTable

Return type:

MaskedArray

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Return type:

TimeSeriesCube

  • dimensions (list[Dimension])

  • values (ndarray | MaskedArray)

  • frequency (Frequency)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

classmethod from_timeseries_list(series, dimension, *, frequency=None, timezone=None, name=None, unit=None, description=None, data_type=None, attributes=None)[source]
Return type:

TimeSeriesCube

Parameters:

  • series (list)

  • dimension (Dimension)

  • frequency (Frequency | None)

  • timezone (str | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • unit (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • data_type (DataType | None)

  • attributes (dict[str, str] | None)

coverage_bar()[source]
Return type:

CoverageBar

equals(other)[source]
Return type:

bool

Parameters:

other (object)

Dimension

class timedatamodel.Dimension(name, labels)[source]
Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • labels (list[datetime] | list[float] | list[str])

name: str
labels: list[datetime] | list[float] | list[str]

TimeSeriesCollection

class timedatamodel.TimeSeriesCollection(series=None, *, name=None, description=None)[source]

Container for TimeSeries and/or TimeSeriesTable objects that don’t share an index.

Items are stored internally as an ordered dict[str, TimeSeries | TimeSeriesTable].

Parameters:

  • series (list[TimeSeries | TimeSeriesTable] | dict[str, TimeSeries | TimeSeriesTable] | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

property name: str | None
property description: str | None
property names: list[str]
property series_count: int
keys()[source]
values()[source]
items()[source]
add(item, name=None)[source]
Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

  • item (TimeSeries | TimeSeriesTable)

  • name (str | None)

remove(name)[source]
Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

name (str)

filter_by_location(center, radius_km)[source]

Keep series within radius_km of center.

Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

  • center (GeoLocation)

  • radius_km (float)

filter_by_area(area)[source]

Keep series inside area.

Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

area (GeoArea)

nearest(target, n=1)[source]

Keep the n nearest series to target.

Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

  • target (GeoLocation)

  • n (int)

to_pandas_dataframe()[source]

Outer-join all series into a single pandas DataFrame.

Each series becomes a column named by its key. The index is the union of all timestamps (outer join), with NaN for missing values.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

to_pd_df()[source]

Alias for to_pandas_dataframe().

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

property df: pd.DataFrame

Shorthand for to_pandas_dataframe().

to_polars_dataframe()[source]

Outer-join all series into a single polars DataFrame.

to_pl_df()[source]

Alias for to_polars_dataframe().

to_numpy()[source]

Return each series as a numpy array in a dict keyed by series name.

Return type:

dict[str, np.ndarray]

property arr: dict[str, np.ndarray]

Shorthand for to_numpy().

coverage_bar()[source]

Return a multi-row CoverageBar spanning the global time range.

Return type:

CoverageBar

Frequency

class timedatamodel.Frequency(*values)[source]
P1Y = 'P1Y'
P3M = 'P3M'
P1M = 'P1M'
P1W = 'P1W'
P1D = 'P1D'
PT1H = 'PT1H'
PT30M = 'PT30M'
PT15M = 'PT15M'
PT10M = 'PT10M'
PT5M = 'PT5M'
PT1M = 'PT1M'
PT1S = 'PT1S'
NONE = 'NONE'
property is_calendar_based: bool
to_timedelta()[source]
Return type:

timedelta | None

DataType

class timedatamodel.TimeSeriesType(*values)[source]

GeoLocation

Parameters:

  • latitude (float)

  • longitude (float)

latitude: float
longitude: float
distance_to(other, unit='km')[source]

Haversine great-circle distance to other.

Return type:

float

Parameters:

  • other (GeoLocation)

  • unit (str)

bearing_to(other)[source]

Initial bearing in degrees [0, 360) from self to other.

Return type:

float

Parameters:

other (GeoLocation)

midpoint(other)[source]

Geographic midpoint on the great circle.

Return type:

GeoLocation

Parameters:

other (GeoLocation)

offset(distance_km, bearing_deg)[source]

New point displaced by distance_km along bearing_deg.

Return type:

GeoLocation

Parameters:

  • distance_km (float)

  • bearing_deg (float)

is_within(area)[source]

True if this point is inside area.

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

area (GeoArea)

GeoArea

class timedatamodel.GeoArea(polygon, name=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • polygon (Polygon)

  • name (str | None)

polygon: Polygon
name: str | None
property bounds: tuple[float, float, float, float]
property centroid: GeoLocation
classmethod from_coordinates(coords, name=None)[source]

Create a GeoArea from a list of (lat, lon) coordinate pairs.

Return type:

GeoArea

Parameters:

  • coords (list[tuple[float, float]])

  • name (str | None)

contains_point(location)[source]

True if location is inside this area.

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

location (GeoLocation)

contains_area(other)[source]

True if other is entirely inside this area.

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

other (GeoArea)

overlaps(other)[source]

True if this area and other share any space.

Return type:

bool

Parameters:

other (GeoArea)

distance_to(other)[source]

Approximate distance in km (centroid-based Haversine).

Returns 0.0 if the point is contained or the areas overlap.

Return type:

float

Parameters:

other (GeoLocation | GeoArea)

classmethod bounding_box(center, radius_km, name=None)[source]

Create a rectangular area centered on center with half-side radius_km.

Return type:

GeoArea

Parameters:

  • center (GeoLocation)

  • radius_km (float)

  • name (str | None)

AggregationMethod

class timedatamodel.AggregationMethod(*values)[source]
SUM = 'sum'
MEAN = 'mean'
MIN = 'min'
MAX = 'max'

HierarchyNode

class timedatamodel.HierarchyNode(key, level, children=<factory>, timeseries=None, location=None, _parent=None)[source]
Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • level (str)

  • children (list[HierarchyNode])

  • timeseries (TimeSeries | None)

  • location (GeoLocation | GeoArea | None)

  • _parent (HierarchyNode | None)

key: str
level: str
children: list[HierarchyNode]
timeseries: TimeSeries | None
location: GeoLocation | GeoArea | None
property is_leaf: bool
property parent: HierarchyNode | None
property siblings: list[HierarchyNode]
property depth: int
property path: list[str]
property leaf_count: int

HierarchyTree

class timedatamodel.HierarchyTree(root)[source]

Displayable tree visualization for a HierarchicalTimeSeries.

Parameters:

root (HierarchyNode)

HierarchicalTimeSeries

class timedatamodel.HierarchicalTimeSeries(root, *, name=None, description=None, aggregation=AggregationMethod.SUM, levels=None)[source]

Bases: object

A tree of time series organised into named hierarchy levels.

Parameters:

  • root (HierarchyNode)

  • name (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • aggregation (AggregationMethod)

  • levels (list[str] | None)

classmethod from_dict(tree, series_map, *, levels=None, name=None, description=None, aggregation=AggregationMethod.SUM)[source]

Build from a nested dict and a flat series mapping.

Example:

tree = {"Norway": {"Bergen": "bergen_ts", "Oslo": "oslo_ts"}}
series_map = {"bergen_ts": ts_bergen, "oslo_ts": ts_oslo}
Return type:

HierarchicalTimeSeries

Parameters:

  • tree (dict)

  • series_map (dict[str, TimeSeries])

  • levels (list[str] | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • aggregation (AggregationMethod)

classmethod from_dataframe(df, level_columns, value_column, timestamp_column=None, *, name=None, description=None, aggregation=AggregationMethod.SUM, frequency=None, timezone='UTC')[source]

Build from a long-format pandas DataFrame with hierarchy columns.

Return type:

HierarchicalTimeSeries

Parameters:

  • level_columns (list[str])

  • value_column (str)

  • timestamp_column (str | None)

  • name (str | None)

  • description (str | None)

  • aggregation (AggregationMethod)

  • timezone (str)

property root: HierarchyNode
property name: str | None
property description: str | None
property levels: list[str]
property n_levels: int
property n_leaves: int
property n_nodes: int
property frequency: Frequency
property timezone: str
property unit: str | None
property begin: datetime | None
property end: datetime | None
get_node(*path)[source]

Navigate to a node by key path.

Return type:

HierarchyNode

Parameters:

path (str)

get_level(level)[source]

All nodes at a given level name or depth index.

Return type:

list[HierarchyNode]

Parameters:

level (str | int)

leaves()[source]

All leaf nodes.

Return type:

list[HierarchyNode]

walk(order='pre')[source]

Yield nodes in pre-order or post-order.

Return type:

Iterator[HierarchyNode]

Parameters:

order (str)

subtree(*path)[source]

Extract sub-hierarchy rooted at the given path.

Return type:

HierarchicalTimeSeries

Parameters:

path (str)

aggregate(node=None, method=None, auto_align=False)[source]

Recursive bottom-up aggregation.

Return type:

TimeSeries

Parameters:

  • node (HierarchyNode | None)

  • method (AggregationMethod | None)

  • auto_align (bool)

aggregate_level(level, method=None, auto_align=False)[source]

Aggregate every node at level.

Return type:

dict[str, TimeSeries]

Parameters:

  • level (str | int)

  • method (AggregationMethod | None)

  • auto_align (bool)

to_collection(level=None)[source]

Flatten to a TimeSeriesCollection.

Return type:

TimeSeriesCollection

Parameters:

level (str | int | None)

to_table(level=None)[source]

Flatten to a TimeSeriesTable (requires shared timestamps).

Return type:

TimeSeriesTable

Parameters:

level (str | int | None)

tree()[source]

Return a displayable tree visualization.

Return type:

HierarchyTree
